channel.title: NHC Atlantic
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Atlantic, Caribbean, and the Gulf of America

item.title: Atlantic Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
390
ABNT20 KNHC 221742
TWOAT

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
200 PM EDT Wed Jul 22 2026

For the North Atlantic...Caribbean Sea and the Gulf of America:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Bertha, located over the northern Gulf of America.

Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

$$
Forecaster Reinhart
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Bertha (AT2/AL022026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...CENTER OF BERTHA MOVING WESTWARD OVER SOUTHEASTERN LOUISIANA... ...TROPICAL STORM WARNING EXTENDED WESTWARD ACROSS PORTIONS OF SOUTHWESTERN LOUISIANA... As of 4:00 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 the center of Bertha was located near 29.8, -89.8 with movement W at 7 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1000 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 45 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Bertha
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...CENTER OF BERTHA MOVING WESTWARD OVER SOUTHEASTERN LOUISIANA... ...TROPICAL STORM WARNING EXTENDED WESTWARD ACROSS PORTIONS OF SOUTHWESTERN LOUISIANA...

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Public Advisory Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 400 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026 
000
WTNT32 KNHC 222047
TCPAT2
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Bertha Advisory Number  14
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
400 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026
 
...CENTER OF BERTHA MOVING WESTWARD OVER SOUTHEASTERN LOUISIANA...
...TROPICAL STORM WARNING EXTENDED WESTWARD ACROSS PORTIONS OF
SOUTHWESTERN LOUISIANA...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 400 PM CDT...2100 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...29.8N 89.8W
ABOUT 25 MI...35 KM SE OF NEW ORLEANS LOUISIANA
ABOUT 70 MI...110 KM SW OF BILOXI MISSISSIPPI
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...45 MPH...75 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...W OR 270 DEGREES AT 7 MPH...11 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1000 MB...29.53 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
CHANGES WITH THIS ADVISORY:
 
The Tropical Storm Warning has been extended westward to Cameron,
Louisiana.
 
SUMMARY OF WATCHES AND WARNINGS IN EFFECT:
 
A Tropical Storm Warning is in effect for...
* Alabama/Florida border to Cameron, Louisiana
* Metropolitan New Orleans and Lake Pontchartrain
 
A Tropical Storm Watch is in effect for...
* West of Cameron, Louisiana, to Sargent, Texas
* Lake Maurepas
 
A Tropical Storm Warning means that tropical storm conditions are
expected somewhere within the warning area, in this case within 24
hours.
 
A Tropical Storm Watch means that tropical storm conditions are
possible within the watch area, in this case within the next 24 to
36 hours.
 
For storm information specific to your area, including possible
inland watches and warnings, please monitor products issued by your
local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 400 PM CDT (2100 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Bertha was
located near latitude 29.8 North, longitude 89.8 West. Bertha is
moving toward the west near 7 mph (11 km/h). A faster westward
motion is expected during the next day or so. On the forecast track,
the center of Bertha will move near or along the Louisiana and upper
Texas coasts through Thursday, and then move farther inland into
Texas Thursday night.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 45 mph (75 km/h) with higher gusts.
Little change in strength is expected during the next day or so,
and Bertha is forecast to maintain tropical storm intensity up
until it reaches the upper Texas coast on Thursday. Bertha is
expected to dissipate inland over Texas by Thursday night or Friday.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 160 miles (260 km)
from the center. A sustained wind of 36 mph (58 km/h) and a gust to 
44 mph (71 km/h) were recently reported at New Orleans Lakefront 
Airport.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1000 mb (29.53 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
Key messages for Tropical Storm Bertha can be found in the Tropical
Cyclone Discussion under AWIPS header MIATCDAT2 and WMO header
WTNT42 KNHC.
 
WIND:  Tropical storm conditions will continue spreading across
portions of the northern Gulf Coast within the warning area through
Thursday. Tropical storm conditions are possible within the watch
area by Thursday or Thursday night.
 
STORM SURGE:  The combination of storm surge and the tide will
cause normally dry areas near the immediate coast to be flooded by
rising waters moving inland from the shoreline. The water could
reach the following heights above ground somewhere in the indicated
areas if the peak surge occurs at the time of high tide...
 
Alabama/Florida border to Port Bolivar, TX...1-3 ft
Lake Pontchartrain...1-3 ft
 
The deepest water will occur along the immediate coast.
Surge-related flooding depends on the relative timing of the surge
and the tidal cycle, and can vary greatly over short distances. For
information specific to your area, please see products issued by
your local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
For a complete depiction of areas at risk of storm surge inundation,
please see the National Weather Service Peak Storm Surge Graphic,
available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?peakSurge.
 
RAINFALL: Bertha is expected to produce total rainfall amounts of 1
to 4 inches with isolated higher totals of 6 inches through Friday
along the central to western Gulf Coast from Northwest Florida to
southern Texas. This rainfall may produce isolated flash flooding,
especially in urban areas.
 
For a complete depiction of forecast rainfall associated with
Bertha, please see the National Weather Service Storm Total Rainfall
Graphic available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?rainqpf and
the Flash Flood Risk graphic at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ero.
 
SURF: Swells generated by Bertha will continue spreading westward
along the northern Gulf Coast during the next few days. These swells
are likely to cause life-threatening surf and rip current
conditions. Please consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next intermediate advisory at 700 PM CDT.
Next complete advisory at 1000 PM CDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Berg
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Advisory Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC WED JUL 22 2026 
000
WTNT22 KNHC 222046
TCMAT2
 
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  14
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026
2100 UTC WED JUL 22 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.8N  89.8W AT 22/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST OR 270 DEGREES AT   6 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1000 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  40 KT WITH GUSTS TO  50 KT.
34 KT....... 50NE 140SE 140SW  30NW.
4 M SEAS.... 90NE 120SE   0SW   0NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.8N  89.8W AT 22/2100Z
AT 22/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 29.9N  89.2W
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0600Z 29.6N  91.3W...NEAR THE COAST
MAX WIND  40 KT...GUSTS  50 KT.
34 KT... 40NE 130SE 120SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1800Z 29.5N  93.9W...OVER WATER
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT... 40NE 100SE   0SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 29.4N  96.9W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  25 KT...GUSTS  35 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 29.8N  89.8W
 
INTERMEDIATE PUBLIC ADVISORY...WTNT32 KNHC/MIATCPAT2...AT 23/0000Z
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 23/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER BERG
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Discussion Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 400 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026 
321 
WTNT42 KNHC 222048
TCDAT2
 
Tropical Storm Bertha Discussion Number  14
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
400 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026
 
Bertha's center made landfall on the eastern side of St. Bernard
Parish, Louisiana, around 2 pm CDT. The main part of the
circulation remains devoid of deep convection, although there is a
large and persistent convective complex ongoing south of the
Louisiana coast. Based on flight-level data from an earlier Air
Force Reserve Hurricane Hunter flight and scatterometer data, the
maximum winds are estimated to be 40 kt, primarily in a band well
to the south and southeast of the center over the Gulf waters.
These winds are likely to begin spreading onshore over parts of 
extreme southeastern Louisiana later this evening as Bertha 
continues moving westward.
 
After moving west-northwestward since last night, Bertha's center
appears to have resumed a westward motion over the past few hours.
The short-term track forecast is intriguing, with just about every
global model suggesting that the center will take a dip or re-form
to the southwest along the southern Louisiana coast as early as
this evening or tonight. Thereafter, the storm is expected to track
westward, moving very near the southwestern coast of Louisiana to
the upper Texas coast by Thursday afternoon. Despite this morning's
northward jog, the updated NHC track forecast is very close to the
previous prediction and is near the middle of the guidance
envelope. In this particular case, the forecast is a bit south of
the HCCA and GDMI guidance, which probably wouldn't handle a center
re-formation as well as the global models.
 
Model fields suggest that the prominent band of
tropical-storm-force winds to the east and south of the center will
persist through Thursday, and therefore only minimal weakening is
expected over the next 24 hours. The NHC forecast maintains Bertha
as a tropical storm for about the next 24 hours until the center
reaches the upper Texas coast. Global model fields suggest the
surface circulation will disintegrate shortly after the center
moves onshore the Texas coast. A remnant low forecast position is
provided inland over Texas at 36 hours, but it's possible the
system will have dissipated by then.

 
Key Messages:
 
1. Tropical storm conditions are expected to spread westward along
the northern Gulf Coast from Alabama to southwestern Louisiana,
including the Metro New Orleans area, tonight and on Thursday.
Tropical storm conditions are possible for the extreme southwestern
coast of Louisiana and upper Texas coast on Thursday.
 
2. A Coastal Flood Warning is in effect for the Mississippi and
Louisiana coastlines, where moderate coastal flooding is expected.
 
3. A risk for isolated flash flooding shifts west along the Gulf
Coast from Northwest Florida to southern Texas through Thursday
night.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  22/2100Z 29.8N  89.8W   40 KT  45 MPH...INLAND
 12H  23/0600Z 29.6N  91.3W   40 KT  45 MPH...NEAR THE COAST
 24H  23/1800Z 29.5N  93.9W   35 KT  40 MPH...OVER WATER
 36H  24/0600Z 29.4N  96.9W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 48H  24/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Berg
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Wind Speed Probabilities Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC WED JUL 22 2026 
132 
FONT12 KNHC 222047
PWSAT2
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  14           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026               
2100 UTC WED JUL 22 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 2100Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 29.8 NORTH...LONGITUDE 89.8 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED    
WINDS NEAR 40 KTS...45 MPH...75 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   ATLANTIC STANDARD TIME (AST)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   EASTERN  DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   CENTRAL  DAYLIGHT TIME (CDT)...SUBTRACT 5 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        18Z WED AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 06Z FRI 18Z FRI 18Z SAT 18Z SUN
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             06Z THU 18Z THU 06Z FRI 18Z FRI 18Z SAT 18Z SUN 18Z MON
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
GULFPORT MS    34 81   X(81)   X(81)   X(81)   X(81)   X(81)   X(81)
 
STENNIS MS     34 65   X(65)   X(65)   X(65)   X(65)   X(65)   X(65)
 
BURAS LA       34 99   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)
 
GFAM 280N 890W 34 90   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)   X(90)
 
NEW ORLEANS LA 34 95   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)   X(95)
 
HOUMA LA       34 99   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)
HOUMA LA       50  3   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
GFAM 280N 910W 34 36   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)
 
BATON ROUGE LA 34 15   1(16)   X(16)   X(16)   X(16)   X(16)   X(16)
 
MORGAN CITY LA 34 79   2(81)   X(81)   X(81)   X(81)   X(81)   X(81)
 
LAFAYETTE LA   34 13   7(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)
 
NEW IBERIA LA  34 27   9(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)   X(36)
 
GFAM 280N 930W 34  2  18(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)
 
LAKE CHARLES   34  1   9(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)
 
CAMERON LA     34  1  40(41)   1(42)   X(42)   X(42)   X(42)   X(42)
CAMERON LA     50  X   3( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
KOUNTZE TX     34  X   3( 3)   2( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
PORT ARTHUR TX 34  X  11(11)   1(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)
 
GALVESTON TX   34  X  20(20)  13(33)   X(33)   X(33)   X(33)   X(33)
 
HOUSTON TX     34  X   2( 2)   8(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)
 
FREEPORT TX    34  X   5( 5)  18(23)   X(23)   X(23)   X(23)   X(23)
 
GFAM 280N 950W 34  X   5( 5)   5(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)
 
HIGH ISLAND TX 34  X  19(19)   4(23)   X(23)   X(23)   X(23)   X(23)
 
MATAGORDA TX   34  X   1( 1)  17(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)
 
PORT O CONNOR  34  X   X( X)  11(11)   X(11)   X(11)   X(11)   X(11)
 
ROCKPORT TX    34  X   X( X)   3( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER BERG
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Public Advisory Update Number
item.description:
Issued at 200 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026 
453 
WTNT62 KNHC 221901
TCUAT2
 
Tropical Storm Bertha Tropical Cyclone Update
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
200 PM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026
 
...TROPICAL STORM BERTHA MAKES LANDFALL IN ST. BERNARD PARISH, 
LOUISIANA...
 
Satellite images indicate that the center of Tropical Storm Bertha 
has made landfall near the Biloxi Wildlife Management Area in St. 
Bernard Parish, Louisiana. The maximum sustained winds are estimated 
to be 45 mph (75 km/h) with higher gusts.


SUMMARY OF 200 PM CDT...1900 UTC...INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
LOCATION...29.9N 89.4W
ABOUT 45 MI...70 KM SW OF BILOXI MISSISSIPPI
ABOUT 40 MI...65 KM E OF NEW ORLEANS LOUISIANA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...45 MPH...75 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...WNW OR 295 DEGREES AT 7 MPH...11 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1000 MB...29.53 INCHES
 
$$
Forecaster Reinhart/Berg
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
